decisions.title: Default Sampling Methods for Explore V2
decisions.context: ENG-10497 and ENG-10626 propose making min/max default for numeric/boolean/enum channels and changedOnly for unums. This affects how users first interact with large datasets in Explore V2, directly impacting 'Nail the Core' by improving out-of-box experience.

arguments.for: Min/max provides better signal density for numeric data; changedOnly reduces noise for state changes; aligns with user mental models from customer research
arguments.against: Breaking change for existing users; may increase backend load; some edge cases where raw data sampling preferred

decisions.title: Sift-Edge Sync UI Investment Level
decisions.context: ENG-10690 proposes sync page with status/metrics. With ENG-10688, ENG-10692 adding sync capabilities, need to decide if this is MVP or full-featured dashboard. Impacts 'Embed Deeper' strategy for edge deployment customers.

arguments.for: Full dashboard increases customer confidence in edge deployments; visibility into sync health reduces support burden; competitive differentiator
arguments.against: MVP gets to market faster; uncertain customer demand for detailed metrics; engineering capacity constrained with infra reviews (ENG-10671)

decisions.title: Live Mode Implementation Strategy
decisions.context: ENG-7023 (live mode in relative time) and DES-197 (Live Mode UX) converging. Need decision on whether live mode is Explore V2 GA blocker or post-GA enhancement. Affects North Star metric as live monitoring increases daily engagement.

arguments.for: Live mode critical for operational use cases; customers explicitly requesting; increases stickiness and daily decisions captured
arguments.against: Complex engineering lift; relative time support adds edge cases; can ship iteratively post-GA

decisions.title: Feature Flag Migration Timing
decisions.context: ENG-10662 proposes file-based feature flag system. With Explore V2, Canvas, Python Rules all in active development, need to decide migration timing to avoid disrupting releases.
decisions.blockedBy: Resource usage review with infra team (ENG-10671) must complete first to understand system capacity

arguments.for: File-based system improves local development; reduces external dependencies; better version control
arguments.against: Migration risk during active feature development; requires coordination across all teams; potential deployment complexity

decisions.title: Athena MCP Integration Scope
decisions.context: ENG-10669 proposes Athena MCP (Model Context Protocol). Unclear if this is exploratory prototype or production integration. Impacts 'Build the Moat' via AI-assisted decision-making, but competes with Ask SIFT roadmap.

arguments.for: MCP enables LLM integration without custom infrastructure; unlocks new AI interaction patterns; industry standard emerging
arguments.against: Adds new architectural dependency; unclear customer demand vs Ask SIFT; may duplicate capabilities

timeAudit.weekSummary: 62% meeting load - above healthy threshold. Recommend consolidating Explore V2 standup + sync into single session.
alerts: Back-to-back meetings Wed 2-5pm blocks decision-making time
3 overlapping 1:1s with ENG-10662, ENG-10671, ENG-10669 owners - consolidate into architecture review session
Friday has only 1 focus block - protect for strategic work on Live Mode decision

events.title: Live Mode Strategy Decision Due
